summary: First E2E test run for EduSense. Backend: 23/23 pytest tests pass after 1 fix. Frontend: All 6 role logins work, student dashboard/quiz/AI Tutor (real Gemini SSE streaming) /adaptive test with reasons /profile all render; teacher record-teaching workflow (Math -> Fractions -> Basic Fractions -> Mark as Taught) creates record; parent/school/company/gov dashboards render KPIs, charts and lists correctly.

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to nested ObjectId inside stored assessment.questions (Motor injected _id into embedded question docs during insert_one of adaptive test). FIXED in server.py via recursive _strip_ids() helper.

integration_issues.issue: Navigates to /student/tutor/{topicId} (with practice mode) rather than /student/quiz/{topicId}. Review request lists 'weak topics with practice buttons' without specifying target route — this may be by design (Tutor gives 2 practice questions). Quiz route is reachable at /student/quiz/{topicId} directly and works.

action_items: Confirm expected behavior of 'Practice' button on weak topics — should it open Quiz or AI Tutor? Currently opens Tutor.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py `test.pop('_id', None)` in generate_adaptive_test is a no-op (test dict never gets _id — insert_one mutates the passed dict(test) copy). Real _id leak was in nested questions list; fix landed with _strip_ids helper for upcoming-assessments endpoint. Recommend also stripping nested _id in generate_adaptive_test response for defensive consistency.
server.py getStreak increment `if score >= 60` fires on every quiz submit — could inflate streak on same-day repeated submissions (no per-day dedupe).
server.py uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI; switch to lifespan handlers.

Frontend AITutor: on mount user message 'Explain this topic to me.' appears twice initially (likely React StrictMode double-invocation of effect). Consider guarding with a ref to avoid duplicate initial stream.
test_credentials: All demo users (student@demo.com etc.) with password 'demo123' as documented in /app/memory/test_credentials.md
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py, runs via `pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py`. It covers auth (6 roles), curriculum, student today/profile/upcoming, quiz get+submit, real Gemini SSE tutor stream (verifies delta+done), adaptive test generate+submit, teacher overview + teaching-records create, parent child, school/company/government overviews. AI Tutor React component causes a double initial 'Explain this topic to me.' user message due to StrictMode-like re-invoke; not blocking but visible.
rca of the issue: upcoming-assessments 500: `generate_adaptive_test` inserts the assessment via `db.assessments.insert_one(dict(test))`. Even though the initial questions were fetched with projection {'_id': 0}, Motor's insert_one adds ObjectId `_id` fields into embedded question sub-documents (behavior of pymongo when inserting docs whose nested items pass through BSON encoding of dicts referenced by the shallow copy). On subsequent GET, the projection {'_id': 0} only strips the top-level _id. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder then fails on nested ObjectId. Mitigation: recursive _strip_ids() applied before returning.
